summary: The PoC bundle was generated to demonstrate GET-based CSRF bypass for organization member removal. The reproduction builds Gogs from source, creates users and an organization, and demonstrates that a GET request removes an org member without CSRF token validation.
expected_security_impact: After running the reproduction, a GET request to /org/:org/members/action/remove?uid=<id> should remove the victim user from the organization without requiring any CSRF token, confirming that state-changing operations are performed via GET without CSRF protection.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction was not executed because the Docker base image (golang:alpine3.21) could not be pulled in the available environment.
verification_method: The exploit script (exploit.py) performs the following steps: (1) sets up Gogs with an admin user, (2) creates an organization, (3) creates a victim user and adds them to the organization, (4) verifies the victim is a member, (5) sends a GET request to /org/:org/members/action/remove?uid=<id> as the org owner, (6) verifies the victim was removed. Success is confirmed by checking that the victim username no longer appears on the org members page.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker base image pull timed out in the execution environment, preventing the PoC from being built and run.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the local target repository and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
